zh.title: ChatGPT 用户大迁移重塑 AI 竞争格局
zh.subtitle: Claude 登顶 App Store 但服务连续宕机，开发者需重新评估依赖策略
zh.tldr: OpenAI 签署 2 亿美元五角大楼合同引发用户抵制潮，ChatGPT 卸载量飙升 295%，Claude 首次登顶美区 App Store，但 Anthropic 因需求激增遭遇多次大规模宕机，暴露了单一 AI 供应商的风险。

keyMetrics.subject: ChatGPT vs Claude 用户迁移数据

rows.metric: 免费用户增长（年初至今）
付费订阅变化（年初至今）

keyMetrics.footnote: QuitGPT 运动称超 150 万人参与行动（取消订阅/卸载/签名）

zh.whatHappened:
2 月 28 日，OpenAI 宣布与美国国防部（现更名为"战争部"）签署价值 2 亿美元的 AI 合作协议。此前 Anthropic 因拒绝国防部要求取消大规模监控和全自主武器使用限制而退出谈判。Dario Amodei 公开表示"不能昧着良心答应他们的要求"。

消息引发大规模用户抵制，ChatGPT 美区卸载量当日飙升 295%，社交平台上"Cancel ChatGPT"成为热门话题，Reddit 相关帖子获 3 万赞。Claude 下载量连续两天大幅增长，首次登顶美区 App Store。

然而 Anthropic 未能承接住这波流量——3 月 2-3 日连续发生 4 次大规模服务中断，累计宕机约 10 小时，波及 claude.ai、API 和 Claude Code。Sam Altman 随后承认合同"看起来很投机和草率"，宣布修改合同增加监控限制条款。
zh.whyItMatters:
这是消费级 AI 市场首次出现因伦理立场引发的大规模用户迁移，标志着 AI 竞争从"谁的模型更强"进入"谁更值得信任"的新阶段。

对开发者而言有三重影响：第一，Claude 的连续宕机证明即使是头部供应商也可能在流量激增时失败，多供应商策略不再是可选项而是必选项；第二，API 服务虽声称未受影响但实际在 3/3 出现了 Opus 4.6 和 Haiku 4.5 的可用性问题，生产环境依赖需要 fallback 机制；第三，AI 供应商的政策风险（军事合作、数据使用等）正在成为技术选型的重要变量。

groups.name: Claude API 开发者
groups.description: 虽然 Anthropic 声称 API 未受影响，但 3/3 的 Opus 4.6 和 Haiku 4.5 模型出现可用性问题。依赖 Claude 的生产服务面临可靠性风险，需立即建立 fallback 机制。

groups.name: ChatGPT API/Plus 用户
groups.description: OpenAI 修改合同增加了监控限制，短期功能和定价未变。但五角大楼合同可能影响 OpenAI 未来的产品方向和数据政策，需持续关注。

groups.name: AI 创业公司
groups.description: 用户迁移浪潮证明 AI 供应商的品牌信任和伦理立场直接影响 C 端留存。使用 OpenAI API 的产品可能面临终端用户的连带信任危机。

groups.name: 个人开发者
groups.description: Claude Pro/Code 在宕机期间完全不可用，影响日常开发工作流。付费用户同样受限于使用配额，高峰期体验可能持续恶化。需要准备备用工具链。

immediate: 为生产环境中的 Claude API 调用增加 fallback 逻辑，当返回 529 错误时自动切换到备用模型（如 GPT-4o 或 Gemini）
检查并导出 ChatGPT 对话历史和自定义指令，以防后续需要迁移
订阅 status.claude.com 的状态更新通知，掌握实时可用性信息
thisWeek: 评估当前 AI 工作流的供应商集中度，制定多模型 fallback 策略文档
测试 Claude、GPT-4o、Gemini 在核心业务场景下的表现差异，建立模型切换的质量基线
审查 AI 供应商的数据使用政策和军事/政府合作声明，将其纳入供应商评估框架
watchAndWait: 关注 Anthropic 基础设施扩容进展，预计 2-4 周内服务稳定性将改善
追踪 OpenAI 五角大楼合同修改后的最终条款和用户流失趋势
观察 Google Gemini 是否趁此机会推出竞争性定价或产能扩展

options.name: 保持现状
options.description: 继续使用当前的单一 AI 供应商，不做架构调整
options.risk: 高——下一次宕机可能直接影响业务

options.name: 多模型 Fallback 架构
options.description: 在应用层实现 Claude → GPT-4o → Gemini 的自动降级链，使用 LiteLLM 或自建路由
options.suitableFor: 有生产 API 调用的团队和产品
options.cost: 1-3 天开发 + 多供应商 API 费用（通常增加 10-20%）
options.risk: 中——不同模型输出风格差异可能影响用户体验一致性

options.name: 全面迁移到 Claude
options.description: 趁 Anthropic 势头将主力从 OpenAI 切换到 Claude
options.suitableFor: 重视品牌伦理对齐、非高可用要求的团队
options.cost: Prompt 改写和测试 2-5 天，Claude API 定价与 OpenAI 基本持平
options.risk: 高——当前 Claude 可靠性尚未验证，且用户涌入可能持续影响服务质量

options.name: 自托管开源模型作为兜底
options.description: 部署 Llama 3 或 Mistral 作为最终 fallback 层
options.suitableFor: 对数据主权和可用性有极高要求的团队
options.cost: GPU 服务器月费 $200-2000+，运维投入较大
options.risk: 低可用性风险，但模型能力与闭源模型有差距

risks.title: Claude 服务稳定性短期内难以保障
risks.description: 用户量激增 60%+ 但 Anthropic 基础设施未同步扩容。3 月初的连续宕机表明其容量规划存在短板。在基础设施追上需求之前，付费用户和 API 调用方都可能持续遭遇限流和中断。

risks.title: 用户迁移热度可能消退
risks.description: 历史上消费者抵制运动通常在 2-4 周后热度大幅下降。Sam Altman 已公开道歉并修改合同条款，部分用户可能回流 ChatGPT，导致 Claude 的增长不可持续。

risks.title: AI 供应商政策风险成为新常态
risks.description: 这次事件表明政府合作、数据使用政策等非技术因素可以在数天内引发大规模用户迁移。任何 AI 供应商的未来政策变动都可能导致类似的信任危机，这是单一供应商依赖的系统性风险。

sources.title: Claude struggles to cope with ChatGPT exodus
sources.excerpt: Forbes 深度报道 Claude 因 ChatGPT 用户涌入导致的服务中断和增长数据

sources.title: Claude outage hits chat, API, vibe coding
sources.excerpt: 3/3 凌晨 Claude 全线宕机超 2 小时，波及聊天、API 和 Claude Code 三大服务。

sources.title: OpenAI's Altman admits defense deal 'looked opportunistic and sloppy'
sources.excerpt: Sam Altman 承认合同仓促，宣布修改条款增加监控限制：AI 系统不得用于对美国公民和国民的国内监控。

en.title: ChatGPT Exodus Reshapes AI Market Dynamics
en.subtitle: Claude hits #1 but outages expose single-vendor risk for developers
en.tldr: OpenAI's $200M Pentagon deal triggered a 295% spike in ChatGPT uninstalls, pushing Claude to #1 on the US App Store. But Anthropic's infrastructure buckled under demand with 10+ hours of downtime, highlighting the fragility of relying on any single AI provider.

keyMetrics.subject: ChatGPT vs Claude User Migration
keyMetrics.footnote: QuitGPT movement claims 1.5M+ participants (cancellations, uninstalls, petitions)

en.whatHappened:
On February 28, OpenAI announced a $200M AI partnership with the US Department of Defense (rebranded as Department of War). This came immediately after Anthropic walked away from the same deal, with CEO Dario Amodei publicly stating he "cannot in good conscience accede to the Pentagon's request" to remove safeguards against mass surveillance and autonomous weapons.

The backlash was swift and measurable: ChatGPT US uninstalls spiked 295% that day, "Cancel ChatGPT" trended across social platforms, and a Reddit post urging users to switch hit 30K upvotes. Claude downloads surged 51% day-over-day, propelling it to #1 on the US App Store for the first time.

Anthropic's infrastructure wasn't ready. Between March 2-3, Claude suffered four major outages totaling roughly 10 hours of downtime, affecting claude.ai, the API (including Opus 4.6 and Haiku 4.5 models), and Claude Code. Sam Altman later admitted the Pentagon deal "looked opportunistic and sloppy" and announced contract amendments adding surveillance restrictions.
en.whyItMatters:
This is the first mass user migration in consumer AI driven by ethical positioning rather than product superiority. It signals a shift from "whose model is better" to "whose values align with mine" as a competitive differentiator.

For developers, three things matter: First, Claude's consecutive outages prove that even leading providers can fail under traffic surges — multi-vendor strategies are no longer optional for production workloads. Second, while Anthropic initially claimed the API was unaffected, Opus 4.6 and Haiku 4.5 both experienced availability issues on 3/3, meaning production integrations need proper fallback logic. Third, vendor policy risk (military contracts, data governance) is now a real variable in technical architecture decisions.

groups.name: Claude API developers
groups.description: Despite initial claims that the API was unaffected, Opus 4.6 and Haiku 4.5 experienced availability issues on 3/3. Production services need fallback mechanisms immediately.

groups.name: ChatGPT API/Plus users
groups.description: OpenAI amended the Pentagon contract with surveillance limits. No immediate product or pricing changes, but the deal may influence future data policies and product direction.

groups.name: AI startups
groups.description: The migration wave proves that vendor ethics and brand trust directly affect end-user retention. Products built on OpenAI's API may face collateral trust damage from their users.

groups.name: Individual developers
groups.description: Claude Pro and Claude Code were completely unavailable during outages, disrupting daily workflows. Usage quotas may tighten further as demand continues to outpace infrastructure.

immediate: Add fallback logic to production Claude API calls — route to GPT-4o or Gemini on 529 errors
Export your ChatGPT conversation history and custom instructions as a backup
Subscribe to status.claude.com notifications for real-time availability updates
thisWeek: Audit your AI workflow's vendor concentration and document a multi-model fallback strategy
Benchmark Claude, GPT-4o, and Gemini on your core use cases to establish quality baselines for model switching
Review AI vendor data usage policies and government partnership disclosures — add these to your vendor evaluation criteria
watchAndWait: Monitor Anthropic's infrastructure scaling progress — expect 2-4 weeks before service stability improves
Track the final terms of OpenAI's amended Pentagon contract and ongoing user churn trends
Watch whether Google Gemini capitalizes with competitive pricing or capacity expansion

options.name: Stay the course
options.description: Continue using your current single AI vendor with no architectural changes
options.risk: High — next outage directly impacts your product

options.name: Multi-model fallback architecture
options.description: Implement automatic degradation chain (Claude -> GPT-4o -> Gemini) using LiteLLM or custom routing
options.cost: 1-3 days development + multi-vendor API costs (typically 10-20% increase)
options.risk: Medium — output style differences between models may affect UX consistency

options.name: Full migration to Claude
options.description: Move primary workloads from OpenAI to Claude while momentum is strong
options.suitableFor: Teams prioritizing ethical alignment, non-HA requirements
options.cost: 2-5 days for prompt rewriting and testing; Claude API pricing is comparable to OpenAI
options.risk: High — Claude's reliability is unproven at current scale, ongoing user surge may degrade service further

options.name: Self-hosted open-source fallback
options.description: Deploy Llama 3 or Mistral as a last-resort fallback layer
options.suitableFor: Teams with strict data sovereignty and uptime requirements
options.risk: Low availability risk, but capability gap vs. closed-source models

risks.title: Claude service stability remains uncertain
risks.description: A 60%+ user surge with no proportional infrastructure scaling led to 4 outages in 2 days. Until Anthropic's capacity catches up with demand, both consumer and API users should expect intermittent rate limiting and disruptions.

risks.title: Boycott momentum may fade
risks.description: Consumer boycotts historically lose steam within 2-4 weeks. Altman has already apologized and amended the contract. Some users will return to ChatGPT, potentially making Claude's growth unsustainable.

risks.title: Vendor policy risk is the new normal
risks.description: This event proved that non-technical factors — government deals, data policies, ethical stances — can trigger mass user migration within days. Any AI vendor's future policy shift could cause a similar trust crisis, making single-vendor dependency a systemic risk.

sources.title: Claude outage hits chat, API, vibe coding
